summary: German artist known for blurry photo-paintings and 'capitalist realism.' Designed pixelated stained-glass for Cologne Cathedral using chance. Birkenau abstract series. Squeegee technique (John Cage series). Fictionalized in Never Look Away.

comprehensive_summary: Gerhard Richter is a German artist known for blurry photo-paintings and 'capitalist realism.' He designed pixelated stained-glass for the Cologne Cathedral using chance. His Met Breuer retrospective 'Painting After All' featured Birkenau abstract works. He uses a squeegee technique (John Cage series). Fictionalized in the film Never Look Away.

cards.front: Artist: German artist known for blurry photo-paintings; designed pixelated stained-glass for Cologne Cathedral using chance
Artist: applies layers of paint then wipes with a squeegee; named a series after John Cage
Artist: fictionalized in Florian Henckel von Donnersmarck's film Never Look Away
